Question Name: What is the clearest interpretation of the broad base in Afghanistan's 2026 population pyramid?
Options: Children form a very large share of the population
Older adults form the largest share of the population
The population has experienced no recent births
Women greatly outnumber men at all ages
Answer: Children form a very large share of the population
Explanation: Children under age 15 make up 42.2% of the population. Successively narrower older cohorts create the classic broad-based pyramid associated with a very young and generally fast-growing population. Income, health care, education and fertility can all matter, so the pyramid is evidence of structure rather than proof of one cause.

Question Name: Which process is most consistent with the narrow base of Albania's 2026 pyramid?
Options: Birth cohorts are much larger than every adult cohort
Birth cohorts have become smaller than the main working-age cohorts
Only males are counted below age 15
Every older cohort has recently immigrated
Answer: Birth cohorts have become smaller than the main working-age cohorts
Explanation: The average width of cohorts aged 0–14 is 0.78 times that of cohorts aged 25–49. A ratio below one indicates smaller recent birth cohorts, often associated with low fertility and possible future aging; economic costs may contribute but cannot be inferred from the chart alone.

Question Name: Which population-pyramid type best describes Algeria in 2026?
Options: A bullet/column profile with similar cohort widths
A low-growth, constrictive profile with a narrow base
A high-growth pyramid with a broad base
A profile dominated by one sex at every age
Answer: A high-growth pyramid with a broad base
Explanation: The average width of the three cohorts aged 0–14 is 1.41 times the average width of the cohorts aged 25–49.
